assessment.recommendation: After perusal of the latest Annual Financial Statements as at 30 June 2024 and in view of the financial summary ratio analysis below, the subject is considered an acceptable risk for your enquiry amount and terms of R 600 000 over 30 days. A maximum credit limit of R 1 500 000 over 30 days is suggested. The account should be reviewed in 6 months.
assessment.profitability: The financial performance is highlighted by the profitability ratios, with a gross profit margin of 39.38% and an operating profit margin of 9.65%.
assessment.liquidity: The subject is considered to be very liquid with a satisfactory current ratio of 1.51 and an acid test ratio of 1.01 respectively.
assessment.solvency: Lastly, the entity's financial position remains stable with an overall solvency ratio of 1.23.
assessment.disclaimer: Please ensure the necessary KYC and Due Diligence is conducted prior to advancing credit or onboarding the customer.

history.narrative: Up and Away Decision Systems CC (1995/987654/23) was established in October 1995. In October 2014, the subject converted to a private company. In April 2020, the name was changed to the current name New Market Decision Systems (Pty) Ltd. In 2022, the assets and operations of Walters and Partners Credit Bureau (Pty) Ltd, were taken over by the subject. Walters and Partners Credit Bureau (Pty) Ltd is now dormant. The remaining 20% shares are held by Goldman Sachs Ltd.

constantUpdateNote: Should any information come to hand in the next 12 months that would change the status of the Company, you will be notified via our "Constant Update Service".

riskGrade.paragraph1: The subject is considered a below average risk, with failure odds of 52.4:1. For every 1 bad company there are 52.4 good companies at this risk band.
riskGrade.paragraph2: Commercial Delphi 4 is designed to predict the probability of business failure over a 12 month period using a combination of adverse, stability and where applicable relevant consumer information in the modelling process.
riskGrade.paragraph3: The model is dynamic, recalculating as and when information affecting the risk grade changes.
riskGrade.poorRiskHeading: Areas influencing a poor risk grade can include:
riskGrade.interpretation: For every 1 bad company there are 52.4 good companies at this risk band.

summary.paymentTrend: This company takes, on average, the same amount of time as other companies within this industry to pay their debt. It can be noted from the payments trend analysis for the last 24 months that the company can be expected to stay within the offered terms of 30 days.
summary.companyStructure: This company is outside of the 53% of BEE for companies within this industry. Consideration should be given to this based on your company policies as BEE companies may be given preference to orders and/or tenders. It is also important to note that this company has a similar number of principals as other companies within this industry. However, pay particular attention to the percentages held by each principal, where applicable.
summary.capacity: Employees, size of fleet and branches determine capacity of a company. This company has less than the norm for companies within this industry. This company operates outside of the same province as 65% of the companies within this industry. The average account size this company requires is higher than the norm for companies in this industry.
summary.historical: Counting in favour of this company is that there is no adverse, judgements or refer to drawer cheques within the last 5 years, showing that this debt can be maintained if managed correctly.
summary.score: This company's score reflects that the risk incurred would be similar to dealing with another company within this industry as 73% score within the average risk category.

mediaAndPressReleases.title: Credit Bureau Expands Services to Regional Markets
mediaAndPressReleases.summary: New Market Decision Systems announced expansion of credit bureau services to regional markets, including bank notifications and data cleaning capabilities.

mediaAndPressReleases.title: FY2024 Annual Results Press Release
mediaAndPressReleases.summary: Financial results for the year ended June 2024 highlight strong performance in credit rating agency activities, with turnover exceeding R188 million.

mediaAndPressReleases.title: Industry Recognition for Credit Data Excellence
mediaAndPressReleases.summary: The subject received recognition for excellence in credit data management and compliance with industry standards.

strengths: Well established with strong trading history since 1995
Strong financial position with satisfactory liquidity and solvency ratios
Account paid on due date with positive trade references
No judgements or adverse court records
Major clients include Absa, Nedbank and Woolworths
weaknesses: Below industry norm for employees and fleet size
Limited capacity compared to industry peers
opportunities: Expansion of credit bureau services to regional markets
Growth in demand for credit data and analytics
threats: Increased competition in credit rating sector
Regulatory changes affecting credit bureau operations
Economic conditions may impact client payment behaviour

amlScreening.status: Clear – no adverse AML indicators identified
amlScreening.summary: Customer due diligence and transaction monitoring screening completed against applicable AML requirements. No suspicious activity or structuring patterns identified for the subject entity.

pepsScreening.summary: Principals and known beneficial owners were screened against domestic and international PEP databases. No individuals were classified as politically exposed persons in terms of the assessed risk policy.

sanctionsScreening.summary: Screening performed against United Nations, major regional and industry sanctions lists. The subject and its principals did not appear on active sanctions or restricted-party lists at the time of enquiry.
